Impact: Catastrophic eruption, VEI 7, created large caldera with Lake Segara Anak, Barujari cone remains active, eruption columns tens of km high, pyroclastic flows burying Lombok and reaching Sumbawa, ashfall in Java
Historical Significance: Triggered volcanic winter, potential role in Little Ice Age, recorded in historical documents, global impact
Geological Features: Large caldera, Barujari cone, eruption columns, pyroclastic flows

Impact: Most powerful eruption in recorded human history, Year Without a Summer in 1816, global temperature decrease, extreme weather, harvest failures, significant climate change, systematic climate forcings
Historical Significance: Most recent confirmed VEI-7 eruption, centuries of dormancy, over-pressurization of magma chamber, violent climax, global ash dispersion

Impact: One of the deadliest and most destructive volcanic events in recorded history, over 70% of the island and surrounding archipelago destroyed, explosion heard 3,110 km away, at least 36,417 deaths attributed to eruption
Historical Significance: Significant global impact, sound heard around the world, tsunamis created, seismic activity reported until February 1884

Impact: Largest and deadliest eruption in New Zealand in the past 500 years, 10-kilometre-high ash column, earthquakes, lightning, explosions heard 500 km away, 17-kilometre-long rift formed, extensive local damage, presumed destruction of Pink and White Terraces
Historical Significance: Deadliest eruption in New Zealand history, destruction of Pink and White Terraces, significant expansion of Lake Rotomahana
Geological Features: Rhyolitic dome volcano, part of Ōkataina Volcanic Centre

Impact: Most significant eruption in the contiguous United States since 1915, declared most disastrous volcanic eruption in U.S. history, phreatic blasts, major explosive eruption, series of earthquakes, steam-venting episodes, large bulge, fracture system, sector collapse, largest subaerial landslide, eruption column, ash deposition, lahars, thermal energy release
Historical Significance: Most disastrous volcanic eruption in U.S. history, significant impact on volcanic studies and hazard mitigation
Geological Features: Large bulge, fracture system, sector collapse, lahars

Impact: Second-largest eruption of the 20th century, ash column 40 km high, pyroclastic flows, caldera formation, global ashfall, $100 million jet damage, lahars, bridge destruction
Historical Significance: Significant global impact, raised awareness of volcanic hazards and disaster response

Impact: One of the largest volcanic eruptions of the 21st century, 3,500 people evacuated, ash cloud spread across cities in the Southern hemisphere, hundreds of flights canceled
Areas Affected by Ash Cloud: Bariloche, Buenos Aires, Montevideo, Stanley, Porto Alegre, Cape Town, Hobart, Perth, Adelaide, Sydney, Melbourne, Wellington, Auckland, Port Moresby

Impact: Largest volcanic eruption since 1991 Mount Pinatubo eruption, caused tsunamis in multiple countries, at least four people killed in Tonga, two drowned in Peru, powerful eruption recorded in modern history, compared to Krakatoa eruption
Historical Significance: Largest explosion recorded in the atmosphere, hundreds of times more powerful than Hiroshima bomb, significant atmospheric disturbance, first eruption since 2014
trigger: 2022 Hunga Tonga–Hunga Ha'apai eruption and tsunami

Impact: End of initial Jōmon culture in southern Kyūshū, parallel to Minoan civilization demise

Pyroclastic flows and lahars from Nevado del Ruiz volcano engulfed town of Armero, killing over 23,000 people, total death toll of 23,000, second-deadliest volcanic disaster of 20th century, led to controversy over government responsibility
Historical Significance: Second-deadliest volcanic disaster of 20th century, raised global awareness of volcanic hazards and disaster response

Impact: Catastrophic eruption, larger than 79 AD eruption, source of Avellino pumice deposits, estimated 3-hour eruption, pyroclastic flows, lithofacies study, catastrophic and widespread impact
Historical Significance: Catastrophic eruption, widespread impact, significant in pottery chronology, remains to be dated precisely
Geological Features: Vent location, pyroclastic flows, lithofacies, deposit thickness

Location: Pacific Ranges of the Coast Mountains, Canada
Geological Features: Bowl-shaped depression, glacial ice and volcanic debris overlay, breached northern rim, lava and ash flows
Geological Processes: Subduction of Juan de Fuca Plate under North American Plate at Cascadia subduction zone

Impact: Deadly eruption in European history, obliterated Roman cities, buried under pyroclastic surges and ashfall deposits, UNESCO World Heritage Site, unknown death toll
Historical Significance: Major tourist attraction, part of Vesuvius National Park, archaeological insights into Roman life

Impact: Largest eruption in last 20,000 years, devastated surrounding area, formed lava domes, caused local tsunamis, expanded lake, massive flood
Historical Significance: Largest eruption in New Zealand, various eruptive styles displayed, significant geological and environmental impact
Geological Features: Rhyolitic lava domes, pyroclastic flow, ignimbrite deposits

Impact: Immense ash-fall over eastern Japan, landslides, starvation, Hokusai's depiction in One Hundred Views of Mount Fuji, formation of new vents, worst ash-fall disaster in Japanese history
Historical Significance: Last confirmed eruption of Mount Fuji, significant impact on Japan, formation of new vents, worst ash-fall disaster in Japanese history
Geological Features: Formation of three new volcanic vents, No. 1, No. 2, and No. 3 Hōei vents

Impact: Plinian eruption, preceded by 7000 years of rest, followed by 4000 years of rest
Catastrophic eruption, destroyed Minoan settlement at Akrotiri, devastated nearby islands and Crete, earthquakes, tsunamis, one of the largest volcanic events in human history
Historical Significance: Marker horizon in archaeological sites, debated date, volcanic lightning possibly described in Egyptian Tempest Stele
Geological Features: Repeated volcanic activity, caldera formation, cyclical eruption process
